terms.definition: Weight in kilograms divided by height in metres squared, used to classify under- and overweight.
terms.exampleSentence: His body mass index of 32 kg/m² places him in the obese class I category.
terms.contextNotes: Less reliable in muscular adults, the elderly and some ethnic groups.

terms.definition: Tape measurement of the arm midpoint used to screen for acute malnutrition.
terms.exampleSentence: A mid-upper arm circumference below 23.5 cm in adults suggests likely undernutrition.
terms.contextNotes: Useful when weight or height cannot be measured (e.g. bedbound patients).

terms.definition: Tape measurement around the abdomen used to estimate central adiposity and metabolic risk.
terms.exampleSentence: Her waist circumference of 92 cm is above the high-risk threshold for women.
terms.contextNotes: Measured midway between the lowest rib and iliac crest, in light clothing.

terms.definition: Ratio of waist circumference to hip circumference; a marker of central fat distribution.
terms.exampleSentence: A waist-to-hip ratio above 0.85 in women indicates increased cardiometabolic risk.
terms.contextNotes: Both measurements taken at end-expiration with the patient standing.

terms.definition: Five-step UK tool combining BMI, weight loss and acute disease effect to score malnutrition risk.
terms.exampleSentence: All adults admitted to the ward are screened with the Malnutrition Universal Screening Tool within 24 hours.
terms.contextNotes: Score 0 low risk, 1 medium, ≥2 high risk requiring dietetic referral.

terms.definition: European nutritional risk screening tool used mainly in hospital inpatients.
terms.exampleSentence: The NRS-2002 is preferred for screening hospitalised adults across most European units.
terms.ipa: /ˌɛn ɑːr ˈɛs tuː θaʊz(ə)nd ən tuː/
terms.contextNotes: Score ≥3 indicates nutritional risk warranting a care plan.

terms.definition: Major plasma protein synthesised by the liver, often used as a marker of chronic illness.
terms.exampleSentence: Her low serum albumin reflected ongoing inflammation rather than dietary protein intake.
terms.contextNotes: Long half-life (~20 days) makes it a poor short-term nutrition marker.

terms.definition: Transthyretin, a short half-life plasma protein sometimes used to monitor acute nutritional change.
terms.exampleSentence: Prealbumin can fall within days of poor intake but is also reduced by inflammation.
terms.contextNotes: Interpret with CRP to separate inflammatory from nutritional drops.

terms.definition: Nitrogenous waste product of protein metabolism cleared by the kidneys.
terms.exampleSentence: A raised blood urea with normal creatinine may suggest dehydration or high protein intake.
terms.contextNotes: Used with creatinine to assess hydration and renal function.

terms.definition: Breakdown product of creatine from muscle, excreted by the kidneys and used to estimate renal function.
terms.exampleSentence: Her serum creatinine has risen, suggesting a fall in glomerular filtration rate.
terms.contextNotes: Levels are influenced by muscle mass, age and ethnicity.

terms.definition: Glycated haemoglobin reflecting average blood glucose over the previous 8-12 weeks.
terms.exampleSentence: His HbA1c of 68 mmol/mol indicates poorly controlled type 2 diabetes.
terms.contextNotes: ≥48 mmol/mol (6.5%) is diagnostic of diabetes in most adults.

terms.definition: Blood test measuring total, LDL and HDL cholesterol and triglycerides to assess cardiovascular risk.
terms.exampleSentence: Her lipid panel showed a raised LDL cholesterol of 4.6 mmol/L.
terms.contextNotes: Non-fasting samples are usually acceptable in primary care.

terms.definition: Spoon-shaped concave nails classically associated with chronic iron deficiency anaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: On examination, koilonychia and pallor pointed towards long-standing iron deficiency.
terms.contextNotes: Also seen in haemochromatosis and some endocrine disorders.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the tongue, often presenting as a smooth red surface in nutritional deficiency.
terms.exampleSentence: Atrophic glossitis can be a clue to vitamin B12 or iron deficiency.
terms.contextNotes: Often accompanied by angular cheilitis in B-vitamin deficiency.

terms.definition: Abnormal accumulation of fluid in interstitial tissues, often pitting on pressure.
terms.exampleSentence: Bilateral ankle oedema raised concern about heart failure or hypoalbuminaemia.
terms.contextNotes: Can confound weight as a nutrition marker; document fluid status.

terms.definition: Structured interview in which a patient reports all food and drink consumed in the previous day.
terms.exampleSentence: She used a 24-hour dietary recall to estimate his usual energy and protein intake.
terms.contextNotes: Quick but subject to memory bias; multiple recalls improve accuracy.

terms.definition: Standardised list asking how often specific foods are eaten over a defined period.
terms.exampleSentence: A food frequency questionnaire helped identify her low intake of oily fish over the past month.
terms.contextNotes: Useful for habitual intake but less accurate for absolute quantities.

terms.definition: Patient-completed record of all food and drink, often kept for 3-7 consecutive days.
terms.exampleSentence: He brought a 7-day diet diary that revealed regular skipped breakfasts.
terms.contextNotes: Weighed records are more accurate but burdensome for patients.

terms.definition: Framework covering Anthropometric, Biochemical, Clinical and Dietary domains of nutritional assessment.
terms.exampleSentence: She structured her review using an ABCD assessment to capture all relevant data.
terms.contextNotes: Some models add 'E' for environmental or social factors.

terms.definition: Wall-mounted or freestanding device used to measure standing height accurately.
terms.exampleSentence: Standing height was recorded using a calibrated stadiometer with shoes removed.
terms.contextNotes: Position the head in the Frankfort horizontal plane during measurement.

terms.definition: Body composition technique that estimates fat and lean mass from resistance to a small electrical current.
terms.exampleSentence: Bioelectrical impedance analysis estimated his body fat at 28%, consistent with central obesity.
terms.contextNotes: Hydration status, recent food and exercise can affect BIA accuracy.

terms.definition: Spring-loaded pincer device used to measure subcutaneous fat at standardised body sites.
terms.exampleSentence: Triceps and subscapular skinfolds were measured with calibrated skinfold callipers.
terms.contextNotes: Operator training is critical to obtain reliable measurements.

terms.definition: Handheld device measuring isometric grip strength as a proxy for functional muscle status.
terms.exampleSentence: Handgrip dynamometer readings were below the age-matched cut-off, suggesting sarcopenia.
terms.contextNotes: Useful at the bedside for monitoring functional change over time.

terms.definition: Set of body measurements such as weight, height and circumferences used in nutritional assessment.
terms.exampleSentence: Routine anthropometry included weight, height, BMI and mid-upper arm circumference.
terms.contextNotes: Use standardised technique and the same equipment for serial measurements.

terms.definition: Bedside clinical tool combining history and examination to grade nutritional status as A, B or C.
terms.exampleSentence: His subjective global assessment was graded B, indicating moderate malnutrition.
terms.contextNotes: PG-SGA is a patient-generated variant used in oncology.

terms.definition: Energy expended at rest in a fasted, thermoneutral state, forming the bulk of total daily expenditure.
terms.exampleSentence: Indirect calorimetry measured a resting metabolic rate of 1450 kilocalories per day.
terms.contextNotes: Often estimated using equations such as Mifflin-St Jeor.

terms.definition: Difference between dietary nitrogen intake and nitrogen losses, mainly in urine, used to assess protein status.
terms.exampleSentence: A negative nitrogen balance suggested that his protein intake was insufficient for recovery.
terms.contextNotes: Requires accurate 24-hour urine collection for nitrogen estimation.

terms.definition: Intracellular iron storage protein whose serum level reflects body iron stores.
terms.exampleSentence: Her serum ferritin of 8 µg/L confirmed iron deficiency in the context of microcytic anaemia.
terms.contextNotes: Acts as an acute-phase reactant; may be falsely normal in inflammation.

terms.definition: Blood glucose measured after at least 8 hours without caloric intake, used to diagnose dysglycaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: A fasting plasma glucose of 7.4 mmol/L on two occasions confirmed the diagnosis of diabetes.
terms.contextNotes: ≥7.0 mmol/L on repeat is diagnostic of diabetes mellitus.

terms.definition: Acute-phase plasma protein that rises rapidly in inflammation and infection.
terms.exampleSentence: A raised C-reactive protein helped explain her low albumin without acute weight loss.
terms.contextNotes: Used alongside albumin and prealbumin to interpret nutrition markers.
